symptoms: Laptop shuts down unexpectedly during heavy workloads. Fan spins loudly before shutdown. System feels extremely hot on the underside.
root_cause: Thermal throttling triggered by dust-blocked heat pipe. CPU junction temperature exceeds 95°C sustained threshold.
resolution:
1. Power off and unplug the device.
2. Use compressed air to clean all ventilation grills (bottom and side).
3. If shutdown recurs, open the bottom panel and re-apply MX-4 thermal paste to the CPU die.
4. In BIOS, set Fan Mode to 'Max Performance' (Advanced > Thermal).
5. If the issue persists, escalate to Hardware Desk with ticket prefix IT-HW.

symptoms: USB peripherals (keyboard, mouse, external drive) randomly disconnect and reconnect. Device Manager shows 'Unknown USB Device (Port Reset Failed)' for the affected port.
root_cause: Corrupted USB selective suspend policy or faulty USB 3.1 host controller driver (version 3.4.x conflict on Windows 11 23H2).
resolution:
1. Open Device Manager → Universal Serial Bus Controllers.
2. Right-click each USB Root Hub → Properties → Power Management → uncheck 'Allow the computer to turn off this device to save power'.
3. Update the USB host controller driver: run 'winget upgrade --id Intel.USB3xHCIDriver'.
4. If unresolved, disable USB Selective Suspend: Control Panel → Power Options → Change Plan Settings → Change Advanced → USB Settings → USB Selective Suspend → Disabled.
5. Reboot and test all ports.

symptoms: Application displays 'License validation failed — server unreachable (ACT-7721)' at startup. Occurs after VPN or proxy change. Offline grace period countdown visible in the tray.
root_cause: ActiveSuite License Server endpoint (licensing.activesuite.corp) is blocked by the new Zscaler proxy policy or DNS is resolving to the wrong IP after a recent network migration.
resolution:
1. Verify DNS resolution: run 'nslookup licensing.activesuite.corp' — expected IP: 10.14.2.55.
2. If DNS is wrong, add a manual host entry: C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts → '10.14.2.55  licensing.activesuite.corp'.
3. Check Zscaler policy: ensure 'activesuite.corp' is in the SSL Inspection bypass list.
4. Clear the license cache: delete 'C:\ProgramData\ActiveSuite\.license_cache'.
5. Restart the ActiveSuite License Agent service (services.msc → ActiveSuiteLicenseAgent → Restart).

symptoms: Intermittent 503 errors on internal web applications. Network icon shows connected but pages time out. Ping to 10.0.0.1 succeeds but ping to 10.0.0.100 (app server) fails. Affects only wired connection; Wi-Fi works fine.
root_cause: NIC duplex mismatch — workstation NIC is negotiating at 100 Mbps half-duplex against a Gigabit switch port, causing heavy packet collision and retransmission which manifests as 503s.
resolution:
1. Open Device Manager → Network Adapters → Nexus Connect NIC → Properties → Advanced Tab.
2. Set 'Speed & Duplex' to '1.0 Gbps Full Duplex' (do not leave on Auto-Negotiation).
3. Flush ARP and DNS caches: run 'arp -d *' and 'ipconfig /flushdns'.
4. On the switch side, request Network Operations to set the port to 'speed 1000, duplex full, no shutdown'.
5. Confirm with: 'ethtool eth0 | grep -i duplex' — expected 'Full'.
